name: Oxygen Therapy
description: High-flow oxygen therapy — the community's #1 abortive. Evidence spans from early case reports to modern RCTs confirming 78% efficacy.

all_trials.title: Oxygen Therapy for Headache
all_trials.sponsor: Aksaray University Training and Research Hospital
all_trials.what_tested: Comparing high-flow and medium-flow oxygen therapy versus placebo for primary headache disorders in the emergency department.
all_trials.key_result: Completed in 2022 with 132 participants but no results posted. Study focused on general primary headaches, not exclusively cluster headache.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This may provide evidence on whether different oxygen flow rates matter for headache relief in the ER, though it was not CH-specific.

all_trials.title: Administration of Oxygen to Cluster Headache Patients
all_trials.what_tested: Comparing three different oxygen delivery systems for acute treatment of cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 57 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: The type of oxygen mask or delivery system you use may matter for how well oxygen works -- this study could help answer that, but results are not yet available.
all_trials.dose_tested: Three different oxygen delivery systems (specifics not detailed)

all_trials.title: Continuous Positive Airway Pressure as a Potential New Treatment for Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Whether CPAP (continuous positive airway pressure, the machine used for sleep apnea) can prevent nocturnal cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 30 participants but no results posted. The idea was based on reports linking sleep apnea to cluster headache.
all_trials.patient_relevance: If CPAP reduces nighttime CH attacks, it would be an affordable and widely available treatment -- but we are still waiting for results.

all_trials.title: A Pilot Study of Demand Valve Oxygen Inhalation Therapy for Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Whether a demand-valve oxygen delivery system (which adjusts flow to breathing rate) works better than standard continuous-flow oxygen for aborting CH attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 4 patients. Results section exists but contains no actual data. Zero adverse events reported.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This study was too small to draw conclusions -- we still do not know if demand-valve oxygen is better than a standard non-rebreather mask.
all_trials.dose_tested: Demand valve oxygen vs continuous high-flow oxygen via non-rebreather mask
